_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

`Youtube` from `lucide-react` is **not valid** in this project’s installed version (`lucide-react@1.0.1`).

I confirmed with type-checking; TypeScript reports:

- `Module '"lucide-react"' has no exported member 'Youtube'.`

So the import in `site-footer.tsx` will fail build/typecheck even though ESLint alone didn’t flag it.

If you want, I can patch it now by swapping in a small inline YouTube SVG (same way the LinkedIn icon is currently rendered), so the footer works immediately.

_**User**_

I want you to add an in the news section at the bottom of the home page with 4 cards for four articles:

as follows:

Article 1: Mandla Money at Apex Developer Conference in Las Vegas
Date 	•	  December 15, 2022

The 2022 edition of the Apex Developer Summit took place in Las Vegas between 6 & 8 September 2022. The event, hosted by Ripple and the XRPL Foundation, is the premier XRPL developer conference that brings together enthusiasts, developers and innovators to showcase and celebrate all things XRP Ledger.
At the event, Julian Kanjere had the privilege of giving a talk titled “Transacting in Low-Tech Environments”. In the talk, Julian showcased how the Mandla Money SMS Wallet enables financial inclusion as it allows transfer of value in low-tech environments (environments without reliable internet and/or smartphones). As blockchain-based wallets and payment solutions take hold, there is an opportunity to advance financial inclusion by adapting the technology from the onset for the low-tech segment of the market — something that the Mandla Money SMS wallet seeks to achieve.

According to the World Bank, there are 2 billion unbanked adults in the world, and there is no reason why this should remain so given we have the building blocks — mobile devices are becoming cheaper and more accessible, blockchain rails are becoming the next iteration of payment rails, and SMS technology has proven to be robust and stood the test of time. The Mandla Money SMS Wallet combines these to offer a solution that can be deployed in any emerging market worldwide. 
By leveraging the XRP Ledger, the wallet offers low transaction fees, fast transaction times, and access to a variety of xrpl-issued digital assets, making it an ideal solution for individuals to receive, transact and store value with ease, where previously they had no access.

Include image in article: apex.png

Article 2: Mandla Money wins XRPL Creating Real World Impact Hackathon
Date: 14 decemember 2022
In July 2022, Mandla Money participated in the XRPL Creating Real World Impact Hackathon hosted by Ripple. Competing against hundreds of competitors, Mandla Money emerged victorious in the Best Social Justice Use Case category for the Mandla Money SMS Wallet’s potential to drive financial inclusion in emerging economies.
The XRPL hackathon which was open to tech teams interested in building apps to improve lives in the real world using any of the XRPL SDKs and APIs consisted of over 300 participants from more than 20 countries. The award categories were Best Social Justice Use Case, Best Economic Use Case and Best Sustainability Use Case.


Mandla Wallet is a digital wallet that allows users to receive, transact and store value using digital assets via SMS, with no need for a smartphone or an internet connection. We see an opportunity for this wallet to drive financial inclusion, reduce frictions with remittances (domestic and regional) and serve as a mechanism for bulk distribution of transparent & traceable relief aid/social welfare at low cost.
We are honoured to receive this award, and grateful to Ripple and the XRPL community for their support and recognition.

Image:xrpl.png

Article 3: Ripple Impact Award for Mandla Money in London
Date 14 decemember 2020

Julian Kanjere from Mandla Money recently received an impact award for work on Mandla Money at the Ripple Impact’s University Blockchain Research Initiative (UBRI) Connect event hosted by the UCL School of Management in London.
Ripple’s UBRI provides students and faculty with strategic guidance, technical resources, and financial support to advance blockchain education around the world. Each year, UBRI hosts the UBRI Connect event which brings scholars, researchers, and thought leaders in the UBRI network to showcase blockchain initiatives, share knowledge, and inspire future research. At this year’s event, various awards were given out, including the 2022 UBRI Impact award which was jointly awarded to Julian Kanjere and Titose Chembezi.


On receiving the award, Julian commented that we couldn’t be more thrilled to receive this recognition for the work towards financial inclusion that the Mandla Money team is putting in, supported the University of Cape Town’s financial innovation hub.
Mandla wallet, which utilities the XRP Ledger, allows users to receive, transact and store value using digital assets via SMS, with no need for a smartphone or an internet connection. This has the potential to accelerate financial inclusion in emerging economies.
Special thanks to UBRI for their efforts towards championing blockchain research and development in academia, and the UCL School of Management for hosting the event.
You can watch the UBRI Connect 2022 Awards and Closing Remarks here:

Image:ripple_award.png 
Induce YouTube video with rewards from the judges: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QZO-NXqYNi4&source_ve_path=NzY3NTg&embeds_referring_euri=https%3A%2F%2Fmandla.money%2F&embeds_referring_origin=https%3A%2F%2Fmandla.money

Article 4: Mandla Money awarded XRPL Grant to Advance Financial Inclusion
Date januaary 2022

We are thrilled to share that Mandla Money has been awarded an XRPL grant from Ripple to further develop our SMS wallet, leveraging the XRP ledger. This grant is a significant step forward in our mission to provide low-cost and easy-to-use digital financial services to underbanked communities in emerging markets. The grant will allow us to achieve a number of important milestones during the course of the year — including security enhancements and integration with the XRPL DEX — with a view to run pilots in various regions.

You can view the official grant announcement here(hyper link to https://xrplgrants.org/awardees?wave=4)

Use image: grant.png

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Good catch — the images had very different aspect ratios, so `object-cover` was cropping them.

I checked actual sizes and updated rendering to avoid cuts:
- `apex.png`: 1024×265 (very wide)
- `xrpl.png`: 300×300 (square)
- `ripple_award.png`: 415×562 (portrait)
- `grant.png`: 642×341 (landscape)

### Fix applied
I switched news-related images from `object-cover` to `object-contain` and added neutral backgrounds so full images remain visible:

- `src/app/page.tsx` (homepage news strip)
- `src/app/news/page.tsx` (news index)
- `src/app/news/[slug]/page.tsx` (article detail)

Now images are fully visible across all articles (no cropping), while still fitting cleanly in layout boxes.

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Fair point — I’ve now done exactly that: **per-article image handling based on the real file dimensions**, not one global style.

### What I changed

I added explicit image metadata in `src/lib/news-data.ts` for each article:
- `apex.png` → `1024x265` (very wide)
- `xrpl.png` → `300x300` (square)
- `ripple_award.png` → `415x562` (portrait)
- `grant.png` → `642x341` (landscape)

Then I updated rendering logic per article:

- **Homepage news strip** (`src/app/page.tsx`)
  - Uses each article’s own width/height values.
  - Renders with natural aspect ratio (`h-auto w-full`) so nothing gets cut.

- **News listing page** (`src/app/news/page.tsx`)
  - Side images now use intrinsic per-article dimensions, no forced crop/cut.

- **Dedicated article pages** (`src/app/news/[slug]/page.tsx`)
  - Added per-article placement strategy:
    - `apex` (ultra-wide): rendered **inline between paragraphs** (best for banner-like ratio).
    - Others: rendered in **side column on desktop**, inline on mobile where needed.
  - Uses natural image sizing to preserve full image.

### Result

- Images are no longer cropped.
- They’re no longer uniformly “shrunk” by a single fixed container rule.
- Each article image is rendered according to its own aspect ratio and best placement.

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Here’s an assessment and proposed plan. **No code changes yet** — review and confirm what you want, then I’ll implement.

---

## Current state (what’s already in the repo)

| Area | Today |
|------|--------|
| **Regulatory content** | Homepage has “Trust & compliance” with 2 short cards (FSP 55523, FIC compliant). About page has similar “Trust & credibility”. Wording is outdated vs your CASP/FIC copy. |
| **Footer legal line** | `© … FSP 55523` only — no CASP line, no Mandla Money Notice block. |
| **Legal docs** | Footer links open PDFs in new tabs (`/documents/*.pdf`). |
| **Floating WhatsApp** | Mobile: pill at `bottom-20`. Desktop: ~220px QR card at `bottom-6`. Footer has no extra bottom padding for overlap. |
| **Hero video** | Already switched to `hero.webm` + `hero.mp4` (~1.2MB each) + `hero-poster.jpg` preload. Older 3.5–3.8MB `video_hero_*.mp4` files still exist but aren’t used in hero. |
| **Partners** | Ripple, Algorand, Tenity, CSIR, RLabs on business page carousel. **Not** XRPL Commons, ZARP, MoneyGram. |
| **Recognition** | Schmidt Futures, Ripple/XRPL hackathon, Tenity $50k, Algorand — amounts/details don’t match your new list. |
| **Personal nav/footer** | No “Savings in USD” or “XRPL Commons Residency”. |
| **Business** | No WaaS or OTC Markets (you flagged WaaS for later). |

---

## Recommendations (decisions for you)

### 1) Licensed & Regulated — homepage vs separate page?

**Recommendation: expand the existing homepage section** (replace “Trust & compliance”), **not** a separate page.

- Trust/regulatory info belongs above the fold journey on a fintech site.
- You already have a section there — upgrading it avoids duplicate content with About.
- Use your **structured card format** (clearer than one long paragraph):

```
Licensed & Regulated
├── FSCA Licensed (CASP, FSP 55523)
├── FIC Registered (Accountable Institution, Reg. 76060)
└── Risk-Based AML/CTF Programme (CDD, sanctions, monitoring)
```

Optional: add `#licensed-regulated` anchor and link from footer badge.

**Also update:** About page trust block + FAQ line that says “FSP 55523” only, so messaging stays consistent.

---

### 2) Footer copyright line

**Proposed line** (fix typo “Provier” → “Provider”):

> © 2026 Mandla Money (Pty) Ltd · Registration 2019/470624/07 · Authorised Financial Services Provider (FSP 55523) · Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP)

---

### 3) Mandla Money Notice

**Recommendation:** compact notice **above** the copyright row in the footer (all pages), with inline links to web legal pages:

> Mandla Money (Pty) Ltd … FSP 55523. Digital assets are not legal tender… Never share your PIN… By using this service, you agree to our Terms of Use, Privacy Policy, and Risk Disclosure.

(Complaints Policy can stay footer-only unless you want it in the notice too.)

---

### 4) Legal docs — embed instead of PDFs

**Recommendation:** dedicated routes, content from PDFs as structured pages:

| Route | Content source |
|-------|------------------|
| `/legal/terms-of-use` | Terms of Use.pdf |
| `/legal/privacy-policy` | Privacy Policy.pdf |
| `/legal/risk-disclosure` | Risk Disclosure.pdf |
| `/legal/complaints-policy` | Complaints Policy.pdf |

- Shared legal page layout (title, last updated if known, readable typography).
- Footer + notice link to these routes (same tab, no `target="_blank"`).
- Keep PDFs in `public/documents/` as optional “Download PDF” if you want — or remove links entirely.

**Note:** I’ll need to transcribe PDF text into MD/TSX (or you provide final copy). ~600KB total PDFs — one-time content work.

---

### 5) Floating QR covering footer

**Root cause:** fixed widget + footer bottom bar with no reserved space.

**Recommended fix (robust, not just nudging `bottom-*`):**

1. **Footer:** add mobile bottom padding (`pb-28` or similar) so copyright + policy links sit above the widget.
2. **Floating widget:** on viewports where QR card shows, either raise it slightly **or** hide/collapse when user is near footer (Intersection Observer).
3. **Mobile pill:** verify `bottom-20` is enough; may need `bottom-24` + safe-area inset for iOS.

This fixes overlap for both the pill and the desktop QR card when the window is narrow.

---

### 6) Slow hero video

Hero is already on optimized assets; if it still feels slow:

| Action | Impact |
|--------|--------|
| Change `preload="auto"` → `preload="metadata"` (poster shows immediately) | Faster first paint |
| Ensure poster is LCP-friendly (already preloaded) | Good |
| Lazy-load video until in viewport (optional) | Saves bandwidth |
| Delete or stop shipping unused `video_hero_0/1/2.mp4` (~11MB) | Smaller deploy, less confusion |
| Compress `hero.mp4` / `hero.webm` further if needed | Lower load time |

**Recommendation:** metadata preload + poster-first; only defer video load if you still see issues after that.

---

### 7) Partner logos

Add to business partner carousel (and optionally a homepage strip if you want):

- **XRPL Commons** (priority — you’ll need logo file or URL)
- **ZARP**
- **MoneyGram**

**Blocker:** logo assets not in `public/visuals/` yet. Plan: you provide PNG/SVG paths, or I fetch official brand assets if you approve.

---

### 8) Personal section links

Add to **footer Personal column** + **header Personal dropdown** (matching existing pattern):

- Savings in USD → `/personal#…` (needs section on personal page or anchor to existing “Save” card)
- XRPL Commons Residency → external URL or `/news/...` if you have a page

Confirm destinations for each.

---

### 9) Business: WaaS & OTC

**Recommendation per your note:** **defer WaaS** (separate product discussion).  
**OTC Markets:** add as business nav/footer item + short section on `/business` unless you want a dedicated page.

---

### 10) Recognition section updates

**Proposed replacement set:**

| Org | Title | Detail |
|-----|--------|--------|
| Ripple Foundation | Grant / support | $50k |
| Schmidt Futures | Reimagine Award | $25k |
| (keep or drop others?) | Hackathon, Tenity, Algorand | TBD |

**Homepage regulatory headline:** align with About — **“Regulated, Licensed, and Built for the Lastmile.”** (you wrote “Lastmile”; About already uses this spelling)

Confirm which old recognition cards to **remove** vs keep.

---

## Proposed implementation phases (after your approval)

### Phase A — Quick fixes (low risk)
- Footer copyright + CASP line
- Mandla Money Notice in footer
- Footer overlap fix (padding + widget positioning)
- Hero video preload tweak
- Recognition copy/amounts update
- Regulatory section upgrade (structured “Licensed & Regulated”)
- Sync About + hero badge text (FSP → CASP where appropriate)

### Phase B — Content & routes
- Create `/legal/*` pages from PDF content
- Point footer + notice links to web pages
- Add partner logos (once assets supplied)
- Personal/Business nav + section updates (except WaaS)

### Phase C — Optional / later
- WaaS as separate product
- Dedicated `/legal` index page
- Remove unused large video files from repo

---

## What I need from you to proceed

Please confirm or adjust:

1. **Licensed & Regulated:** homepage section upgrade (structured cards) — **yes/no**? Separate page instead?
2. **Footer notice:** full block as written — **yes/no**? Include Complaints in notice links?
3. **Legal pages:** transcribe PDFs into site pages — **yes/no**? Keep “Download PDF” secondary link?
4. **Recognition:** replace all 4 cards with Ripple Foundation $50k + Reimagine $25k only, or **add/update** existing?
5. **Partners:** provide logo files for XRPL Commons, ZARP, MoneyGram (or approve sourcing).
6. **Personal links:** URLs for “Savings in USD” and “XRPL Commons Residency”.
7. **Business:** add **OTC Markets** only for now; **skip WaaS** — **yes/no**?
8. **Regulatory headline:** use **“Built for the Lastmile”** everywhere — **yes/no**?

Once you confirm, I’ll implement in the order above.

---

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: pip3 install pypdf -q && python3 << 'PY'
from pypdf import PdfReader
import os
base = "public/documents"
for f in os.listdir(base):
    if f.endswith('.pdf'):
        path = os.path.join(base, f)
        r = PdfReader(path)
        text = "\n".join(p.extract_text() or "" for p in r.pages)
        print("===FILE:", f, "PAGES:", len(r.pages), "===")
        print(text[:3000])
        print("\n---END PREVIEW---\n")
PY</summary>

```bash
pip3 install pypdf -q && python3 << 'PY'
from pypdf import PdfReader
import os
base = "public/documents"
for f in os.listdir(base):
    if f.endswith('.pdf'):
        path = os.path.join(base, f)
        r = PdfReader(path)
        text = "\n".join(p.extract_text() or "" for p in r.pages)
        print("===FILE:", f, "PAGES:", len(r.pages), "===")
        print(text[:3000])
        print("\n---END PREVIEW---\n")
PY
```

```
Mandla  Money  Risk  Disclosure  
 
Effective  Date:  10  March  2026  
This  Risk  Disclosure  outlines  the  key  risks  associated  with  using  Mandla  Money.  By  
using
 
our
 
services,
 
you
 
acknowledge
 
and
 
accept
 
these
 
risks.
 
Mandla  Money  (Pty)  Ltd  (Registration  Number:  2019/470624/07)  is  an  authorised  
Financial
 
Services
 
Provider
 
(FSP
 
55523).
 
 
1.  Nature  of  the  Service  
Mandla  Money  provides  access  to  a  digital  wallet  that  enables  users  to  send,  receive,  
and
 
hold
 
digital
 
assets,
 
including
 
stablecoins.
 
Mandla  Money  is  not  a  bank.  Funds  and  digital  assets  may  be  held  with  or  processed  
through
 
third-party
 
financial
 
institutions,
 
payment
 
providers,
 
or
 
digital
 
asset
 
service
 
providers.
 
 
2.  Digital  assets  are  not  legal  tender  
Digital  assets,  including  stablecoins:  
●  Are  not  issued  or  guaranteed  by  a  central  bank  ●  Are  not  legal  tender  ●  Are  not  covered  by  deposit  insurance  schemes  
This  means  your  funds  do  not  have  the  same  protections  as  money  held  in  a  traditional  
bank
 
account.
 
 

3.  Stablecoin  risk  
Stablecoins  are  designed  to  maintain  a  stable  value,  but  they  are  not  risk-free.  
Risks  include:  
●  Loss  of  peg  to  the  underlying  currency  (e.g.  USD  or  ZAR)  ●  Failure  of  the  issuer  or  underlying  reserves  ●  Liquidity  constraints  in  stressed  market  conditions  
If  a  stablecoin  fails  to  maintain  its  intended  value,  you  may  lose  some  or  all  of  your  
funds.
 
 
4.  Third-party  and  counterparty  risk  
Mandla  Money  relies  on  third-party  providers,  including:  
●  Banks  ●  Payment  processors  ●  Digital  asset  exchanges  ●  Custodians  
These  providers  may  experience:  
●  Operational  failures  or  downtime  ●  Delays  in  processing  transactions  ●  Security  breaches  or  insolvency  
While  we  take  care  in  selecting  and  working  with  these  providers,  Mandla  Money  does  
not
 
control
 
their
 
operations
 
and
 
is
 
not
 
responsible
 
for
 
losses
 
arising
 
from
 
their
 
failure
 
or
 
actions.
 
 
5.  Blockchain  and  transaction  risk  
Transactions  involving  digital  assets  may  be  recorded  on  blockchain  networks.  
Key  risks  include:  
●  Transactions  may  be  irreversible  once  confirmed  ●  Sending  funds  to  the  wrong  recipient  (e.g.  incorrect  phone  number  or  wallet  
address)
 
may
 
result
 
in
 
permanent
 
loss
 ●  Network  congestion  may  cause  delays  or  increased  fees  
Always  verify  recipient  details  carefully  before  sending  funds.  
 
6.  Conversion  and  liquidity  risk  
When  converting  between  local  currency  (e.g.  ZAR)  and  digital  assets:  
●  Prices  may  fluctuate  ●  Exchange  rates  and  spreads  may  apply  ●  Liquidity  may  vary  depending  on  market  conditions  
You  may  receive  less  than  expected  due  to  market  movements, 

---END PREVIEW---

This  Complaints  Policy  explains  how  Mandla  Money  (Pty)  Ltd  (Registration  Number:

We  are  committed  to  resolving  complaints  fairly,  promptly,  and  transparently.  
 
1.  What  is  a  complaint?  
A  complaint  is  any  expression  of  dissatisfaction  relating  to:  
●  The  Mandla  Money  service  ●  A  transaction  or  payment  ●  Our  conduct  or  communication  ●  A  perceived  financial  loss  or  inconvenience  
 
2.  How  to  submit  a  complaint  
You  can  submit  a  complaint  by  contacting  us:  
Email:  complaints@mandla.money  Website: https://mandla.money/ 
Please  include:  
●  Your  full  name  ●  Your  registered  phone  number  ●  A  clear  description  of  the  issue  ●  Relevant  transaction  details  (e.g.  date,  amount,  reference,  or  transaction  ID  
where
 
available)
 

●  Any  supporting  evidence  (e.g.  screenshots  or  confirmations)  
 
3.  What  happens  after  you  submit  a  complaint  
Once  we  receive  your  complaint:  
●  We  will  acknowledge  receipt  within  2  business  days  ●  We  will  investigate  your  complaint  and  may  request  additional  information  ●  We  aim  to  resolve  complaints  within  10  business  days  
If  additional  time  is  required,  we  will  keep  you  informed  of  progress  and  expected  
timelines.
 
 
4.  Resolution  
We  will  communicate  the  outcome  of  your  complaint  clearly,  including:  
●  The  decision  ●  The  reasons  for  the  decision  ●  Any  steps  taken  or  proposed  
Where  appropriate,  we  may  take  corrective  action  or  provide  a  remedy.  
 
5.  If  you  are  not  satisfied  
If  your  complaint  is  not  resolved  to  your  satisfaction,  or  if  it  has  not  been  resolved  within  
6
 
weeks
 
(30
 
business
 
days)
,
 
you
 
may
 
escalate
 
your
 
complaint
 
to
 
the:
 
Office  of  the  Ombud  for  Financial  Services  Providers  (FAIS  Ombud)  
Website: https://www.faisombud.co.za/ 
The  Ombud  is  an  independent  body  that  resolves  disputes  between  financial  service  
providers
 
and
 
clients.
 
Its
 
determinations
 
may
 
have
 
legal
 
effect.

●  Some  transactions,  particularly  those  involving  digital  assets  or  blockchain  
networks,  may  not  be  reversible  ●  Complaints  relating  to  incorrect  recipient  details  or  user  error  may  not  result  in
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
from pypdf import PdfReader
import re, json, os

def clean(text):
    # fix google docs pdf spacing
    text = re.sub(r'\n\s*\n', '\n\n', text)
    text = re.sub(r' +', ' ', text)
    # join broken words: "Mandla  Money" -> keep double space as single
    lines = []
    for line in text.split('\n'):
        line = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', line.strip())
        if line:
            lines.append(line)
    return '\n\n'.join(lines)

docs = {
    'terms-of-use': 'Terms of Use.pdf',
    'privacy-policy': 'Privacy Policy.pdf',
    'risk-disclosure': 'Risk Disclosure.pdf',
    'complaints-policy': 'Complaints Policy.pdf',
}
out = {}
for slug, fname in docs.items():
    r = PdfReader(f'public/documents/{fname}')
    text = clean('\n'.join(p.extract_text() or '' for p in r.pages))
    out[slug] = text
    print(slug, len(text), 'chars')

os.makedirs('src/lib/legal', exist_ok=True)
with open('src/lib/legal/content.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(out, f, indent=2)
print('written')
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
from pypdf import PdfReader
import re, json, os

def clean(text):
    # fix google docs pdf spacing
    text = re.sub(r'\n\s*\n', '\n\n', text)
    text = re.sub(r' +', ' ', text)
    # join broken words: "Mandla  Money" -> keep double space as single
    lines = []
    for line in text.split('\n'):
        line = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', line.strip())
        if line:
            lines.append(line)
    return '\n\n'.join(lines)

docs = {
    'terms-of-use': 'Terms of Use.pdf',
    'privacy-policy': 'Privacy Policy.pdf',
    'risk-disclosure': 'Risk Disclosure.pdf',
    'complaints-policy': 'Complaints Policy.pdf',
}
out = {}
for slug, fname in docs.items():
    r = PdfReader(f'public/documents/{fname}')
    text = clean('\n'.join(p.extract_text() or '' for p in r.pages))
    out[slug] = text
    print(slug, len(text), 'chars')

os.makedirs('src/lib/legal', exist_ok=True)
with open('src/lib/legal/content.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(out, f, indent=2)
print('written')
PY
```

```
privacy-policy 3778 chars
risk-disclosure 4366 chars
complaints-policy 3254 chars
written
```

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Improving PDF text formatting and creating the shared legal, partners, and layout components.


_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
from pypdf import PdfReader
import re, json

def clean_pdf(raw):
    lines = [re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', l.strip()) for l in raw.split('\n') if l.strip()]
    blocks = []
    buf = []
    def flush():
        nonlocal buf
        if buf:
            blocks.append(' '.join(buf))
            buf = []
    for line in lines:
        line = line.replace('\u25cf', '•')
        if re.match(r'^\d+\.\s', line) or line.startswith('Effective Date:'):
            flush()
            blocks.append(line)
        elif line.endswith('information') and len(line) < 30:
            buf.append(line)
        elif line.startswith('•'):
            flush()
            blocks.append(line)
        elif re.match(r'^[A-Z][a-z].*information$', line) and len(line) < 40:
            flush()
            blocks.append(line)
        else:
            buf.append(line)
    flush()
    return blocks

docs = {
    'terms-of-use': ('Terms of Use.pdf', 'Mandla Money Terms of Use'),
    'privacy-policy': ('Privacy Policy.pdf', 'Mandla Money Privacy Policy'),
    'risk-disclosure': ('Risk Disclosure.pdf', 'Mandla Money Risk Disclosure'),
    'complaints-policy': ('Complaints Policy.pdf', 'Mandla Money Complaints Policy'),
}
out = {}
for slug, (fname, title) in docs.items():
    r = PdfReader(f'public/documents/{fname}')
    raw = '\n'.join(p.extract_text() or '' for p in r.pages)
    blocks = clean_pdf(raw)
    # drop duplicate title if first block is title
    if blocks and blocks[0].startswith('Mandla Money'):
        blocks = blocks[1:]
    out[slug] = {'title': title, 'blocks': blocks}

with open('src/lib/legal/content.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(out, f, indent=2)
print('blocks:', {k: len(v['blocks']) for k,v in out.items()})
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
from pypdf import PdfReader
import re, json

def clean_pdf(raw):
    lines = [re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', l.strip()) for l in raw.split('\n') if l.strip()]
    blocks = []
    buf = []
    def flush():
        nonlocal buf
        if buf:
            blocks.append(' '.join(buf))
            buf = []
    for line in lines:
        line = line.replace('\u25cf', '•')
        if re.match(r'^\d+\.\s', line) or line.startswith('Effective Date:'):
            flush()
            blocks.append(line)
        elif line.endswith('information') and len(line) < 30:
            buf.append(line)
        elif line.startswith('•'):
            flush()
            blocks.append(line)
        elif re.match(r'^[A-Z][a-z].*information$', line) and len(line) < 40:
            flush()
            blocks.append(line)
        else:
            buf.append(line)
    flush()
    return blocks

docs = {
    'terms-of-use': ('Terms of Use.pdf', 'Mandla Money Terms of Use'),
    'privacy-policy': ('Privacy Policy.pdf', 'Mandla Money Privacy Policy'),
    'risk-disclosure': ('Risk Disclosure.pdf', 'Mandla Money Risk Disclosure'),
    'complaints-policy': ('Complaints Policy.pdf', 'Mandla Money Complaints Policy'),
}
out = {}
for slug, (fname, title) in docs.items():
    r = PdfReader(f'public/documents/{fname}')
    raw = '\n'.join(p.extract_text() or '' for p in r.pages)
    blocks = clean_pdf(raw)
    # drop duplicate title if first block is title
    if blocks and blocks[0].startswith('Mandla Money'):
        blocks = blocks[1:]
    out[slug] = {'title': title, 'blocks': blocks}

with open('src/lib/legal/content.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(out, f, indent=2)
print('blocks:', {k: len(v['blocks']) for k,v in out.items()})
PY
```

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

All planned changes are now applied. Here’s what was finished in this pass:

### Completed in this session
- **Personal page** — Added `#savings-in-usd` anchor with “Savings in USD” label (matches header/footer nav).
- **Business page** — Shared `partners` list (XRPL Commons, ZARP, MoneyGram, etc.) and new **OTC Markets** section at `#otc-markets`.
- **About page** — Trust section updated with FSP 55523 · CASP · FIC 76060 wording.
- **FAQ & Contact** — Regulatory copy updated to include CASP and FIC 76060.
- **Legal pages** — Fixed URL linkification bug in `legal-document-body.tsx`.
- **Cleanup** — Removed invalid `moneygram.png` and `zarp.png` placeholder files (SVGs remain).

### Already in place from earlier work
- Footer: legal web pages, Mandla Money Notice, CASP badge, social links, mobile padding for floating widget
- `/legal/{terms-of-use,privacy-policy,risk-disclosure,complaints-policy}` routes
- Homepage: Licensed & Regulated section, recognition updates, hero video preload
- Header nav: Savings in USD, OTC Markets
- QR codes → `official_qr.png`, floating WhatsApp desktop QR always visible

### Verification
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — passed
- `npm run build` — passed (18 static pages including all 4 legal routes)

**Note:** ZARP and MoneyGram logos are still SVG placeholders until official assets are available. **WaaS** was left out per the earlier plan. Say if you want that section added or real partner logos swapped in.

The homepage hero uses a `<video>` element in `src/app/page.tsx` with two sources:

1. **Primary:** `public/visuals/hero.webm` (WebM — browsers try this first)
2. **Fallback:** `public/visuals/hero.mp4` (MP4 if WebM isn’t supported)

It also uses **`public/visuals/hero-poster.jpg`** as the poster image (shown before playback and preloaded in `<head>`).

The video is set to `autoPlay`, `muted`, `loop`, `playsInline`, and `preload="metadata"`. The older `video_hero_0/1/2.mp4` files are **not** referenced in the hero.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Reviewing the current setup to identify what's already optimized and what else can be done to improve loading speed.

The video files are already well optimized. They were compressed from roughly 3.5–3.8 MB down to about 1.1–1.2 MB. The poster image is preloaded for instant paint. The metadata preload defers the full download. Both WebM and MP4 formats are provided. The loop is only 8 seconds long.

Several further optimizations are possible. Lowering the resolution to 960×540 or 854×480 would reduce file size without noticeable loss. The bitrate could drop to 500–700 kbps for a subtler background loop. On mobile and slow connections, lazy-loading the video instead of autoplaying would save data. Using prefers-reduced-motion to show only the poster image would help accessibility. Connection-aware loading via navigator.connection or save-data could also skip the video entirely when bandwidth is limited.</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

You're in decent shape already — the hero is **much** leaner than the old unused `video_hero_*.mp4` files (~3.5–3.8MB each). Here's the current state and what would help most.

## What's already optimized

| Asset | Size | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `hero.webm` | **1.1 MB** | VP9, 1280×720, 8s loop — loaded first in modern browsers |
| `hero.mp4` | **1.2 MB** | H.264 fallback — browser only picks **one** source |
| `hero-poster.jpg` | **79 KB** | Preloaded with `fetchPriority="high"` for instant paint |

Good code choices in `page.tsx`:
- **`preload="metadata"`** — avoids downloading the full ~1MB video until playback starts
- **`poster`** — users see something immediately while video buffers
- **`muted` + `playsInline`** — allows autoplay without blocking
- **Short 8s loop** — keeps file size bounded

So yes: it's **reasonably optimized** for a background autoplay video. The main cost is still that ~1MB download on first visit.

## What you can still do (by impact)

### 1. Skip video on mobile / slow connections (biggest win)
The poster at 79KB is enough for a background at 60% opacity with a gradient overlay. On mobile you often pay ~1MB for little visual gain.

```tsx
// Only load/play video on desktop + good connection
const [showVideo, setShowVideo] = useState(false);

useEffect(() => {
  const mobile = window.matchMedia("(max-width: 767px)").matches;
  const saveData = navigator.connection?.saveData;
  const slow = navigator.connection?.effectiveType === "2g" || navigator.connection?.effectiveType === "slow-2g";
  if (!mobile && !saveData && !slow) setShowVideo(true);
}, []);
```

Then render `<video>` only when `showVideo` is true; otherwise keep the poster as a static background.

### 2. Re-encode smaller (easy ~30–50% savings)
At 60% opacity behind a gradient, you can push harder:

- **Resolution:** 854×480 or 960×540 (still fine for full-bleed background)
- **Bitrate:** ~500–700 kbps (you're at ~1.1 Mbps now)
- **FPS:** 24 → 20 if motion is subtle

Example with ffmpeg:

```bash
ffmpeg -i hero.mp4 -vf scale=960:-2 -c:v libvpx-vp9 -b:v 600k -an hero-sm.webm
ffmpeg -i hero.mp4 -vf scale=960:-2 -c:v libx264 -crf 28 -preset slow -an hero-sm.mp4
```

Target: **~400–600 KB** per file instead of ~1.1 MB.

### 3. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`
Users who disable motion shouldn't download or play the video:

```tsx
const prefersReduced = window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches;
```

Show poster only when that's true.

### 4. Smaller poster (minor win)
Convert `hero-poster.jpg` to **WebP or AVIF** — often 79KB → ~25–40KB with no visible change at hero size.

### 5. Remove dead assets from deploy
These aren't used in the hero but still ship with the site:

- `video_hero_0.mp4` (3.8 MB)
- `video_hero_1.mp4` (3.5 MB)
- `video_hero_2.mp4` (3.8 MB)

Deleting them won't speed up the hero, but it **will** shrink your overall deploy and cache footprint.

### 6. CDN caching (hosting-level)
Since this is a static export, ensure your host (Cloudflare, Netlify, S3+CloudFront, etc.) serves `/visuals/hero.*` with long cache headers (`Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`). Repeat visits then cost almost nothing.

## What *not* to do

- **`preload="auto"`** on the video — fights your poster strategy and hurts LCP
- **Preloading both webm and mp4** — browsers only need one
- **HLS/DASH streaming** — overkill for an 8-second loop on a static site

## Bottom line

You're already doing the important things (compressed files, dual format, poster preload, `preload="metadata"`). The next meaningful gains are:

1. **Don't load video on mobile / slow networks** (~1 MB saved)
2. **Re-encode at lower resolution/bitrate** (~300–500 KB saved)
3. **Delete unused `video_hero_*.mp4`** (deploy bloat)

If you want, I can implement the mobile/save-data skip and `prefers-reduced-motion` handling in the hero component.

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Applied the hero performance changes. Here's what changed:

### Smart video loading (`HeroBackground` component)
The hero now shows the **poster image only** by default, and loads the video **only on desktop** when:
- `prefers-reduced-motion` is not set
- Data Saver is off
- Connection is not 2G / slow-2G

Mobile users skip the ~860KB video download entirely.

### Smaller video files
Re-encoded both formats at 854×480:

| File | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `hero.webm` | 1.1 MB | **886 KB** |
| `hero.mp4` | 1.2 MB | **863 KB** |

### Removed unused assets (~11 MB)
Deleted `video_hero_0.mp4`, `video_hero_1.mp4`, and `video_hero_2.mp4` — they weren't referenced but were still deployed.

### Kept as-is
- **Poster preload** in `page.tsx` (79KB JPG — WebP was slightly larger, so kept JPG)
- **`preload="metadata"`** on the video when it does load

Build passes. On a typical mobile visit you should see only the 79KB poster; on desktop you get the smaller video after the poster paints.

Updated the **Our partners** section on `/business`.

The muted look came from `grayscale opacity-60` on each logo link (with color only on hover). That’s removed so logos render in **full color at full opacity**.

The section and each logo tile now use an explicit **white background** (`bg-white` on the section strip and each link wrapper), so transparent PNGs sit on clean white instead of picking up any tint from nearby sections.
